title: Entire floor West Village apartment for rent until mid-July
description: We are immediately subletting our large West Village apartment, fully furnished until mid-July or early August (end date slightly flexible). It is a two bedroom we use as a 1 bedroom with an office and living room, but you could add a bed in the second bedroom. Massive windows overlooking Stonewall, washer/dryer IN UNIT, dishwasher, granite countertops, stainless steel appliances, Brazilian redwood floors. It is above the Christopher Street 1 train, and right around the corner from West 4th Street station A/C/E/ B/D/F/M trains. More details: Our show-stopping home is an entire floor of a historic Greenwich Village building. You'll be directly across from the historic Stonewall Inn and famous jazz clubs. This is the most desired neighborhood in Manhattan, if not the world. Celebrities wander the streets and film shoots are a regular occurrence on our block. You will feel like the main character on your own sitcom, with a stylish apartment to match. Our space is sun-drenched with five massive windows in the living room, and a cozy, back-facing bedroom and separate work space. The Brazilian redwood floors add a flash of color to the MCM aesthetic. If you decide to cook (rather than try one of the hundred cafes and restaurants nearby), the kitchen is modern with granite and stainless steel everything, including a dishwasher. We even have the rarest of NYC amenities: a washer/dryer! The newly redone bathroom includes a smart vanity with inset lights for the perfect makeup application or clean shave. If you are here to work, our generously sized standing desk has two large monitors. We even have an extra monitor you can set on the breakfast bar if you need two work areas. You can get almost anywhere in New York, fast. West 4th street station is a block away, with A/B/C/D/E/F/M trains. The Christopher street 1 train is directly downstairs. 2 PATH stations are also within 8 minutes. You can be in Bryant Park, Times Square, Penn Station, Broadway, Soho, Central Park, and more in less than 15 minutes. You can even get to Hoboken or Jersey City in under 20 minutes, all without a car. Cafes, famous restaurants, comedy clubs, jazz bars and every iconic village landmark are a short walk away. Our apartment offers a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to stay in the heart of it all. All we ask is that you water our friendly house plants. Neighborhood: Greenwich Village is the most iconic place on earth. Comedy clubs, cafes, Stonewall Inn, Jazz bars, normal bars, ghost tours, food crawls, restaurants where you can't get a reservation without selling a kidney. You name it, we have it! Our area is scattered with little parks, instagrammable building façades, and iconic views. You truly won't believe it till you see it. The Marvelous Mrs. Maisel was filmed here. So was Sex in the City. The "Friends Apartment" is a short stroll away, and you might run into Taylor Swift or another Village resident. There's no place like the West Village.

title: Convenient transportation to the east and west side of Manhattan.
description: Simplicity and minimalist style in Crown Heights Brooklyn, 2 blocks from the 2 and 5 trains to both the east and west sides of Manhattan, Walking distance to Prospect Park, Brooklyn Museum, Brooklyn Zoo, and Botanical Gardens. The neighborhood is quiet, diverse and growing with businesses and culture. This is a self-contained unit - part of a three floors English cottage style home. First of all, your private entrance is on the alley to the left side of our house about half way to the back. The entrance is a reddish brown door with a 4 digit access code. We focused on simplicity and lots of attention to details in this space. It is minimalist in all senses. This also means we did fewer finishes and worked with what was in the space, instead of adding cheap materials. You'll find a full-sized bed with a recently purchased very comfortable memory foam mattress for two. If your booking is for one more guest we can provide another twin size mattress (if requested) for an extra charge. The bathroom has recently been renovated with the addition of tile and an enclosed shower. You have 2 sets of shelves on both ends of a built-in closet. The kitchenette is equipped with a few new appliances: hot plate, toaster oven, refrigerator, and small freezer, ceramic drip coffee filter, coffee and tea, and basic cooking and serving essentials. The heating system for the house is behind the built-in closet in the main room. It will need a 10 minute servicing (emptying a few buckets of water from the steam system) every other week, during the winter heating months, so please be aware of this if you consider it too much of an intrusion. It takes about 10 minutes every two weeks. Floors have a white paint patina and protection over a sanded but still not completely leveled cement. Our pets do not have access to your area. Our very sweet and friendly English Mastiff can be noisy when greeting visitors or guests from Airbnb upstairs and when you enter or leave (even though you won't see each other) or on occasion, when playing with us. Prospect Park is a 15 minute walk away, the 2 and 5 subway is two short blocks away on Sterling Street, and there are numerous eateries and coffee shops just short walks away. If you have a car, parking is much easier on the street here than in Park Slope or other neighborhoods. This is an easy and inexpensive way to experience NYC, away from the chaos of the city, but close enough to reach it with a half hour subway ride. We can rent for shorter periods, 160 a night, 672 a week, and 1872 a month. If you want to stay longer term please contact us for a 6 month (or longer) pricing and we'll get back to you to discuss.

Adjacent to it is the brand new, large pass-thru gourmet kitchen, equipped with granite counters, custom cabinetry, stainless steel GE appliances (including a dishwasher, refrigerator, stove top, microwave, and ventilation system).Slightly further up is the full bathroom boasting a marble-tile finish and a large vanity mirror. The large living space is an effortless FLEX-2 conversion, very comfortably. With substantial remaining space for a living quarters, you can decide where to situate any accommodating furniture (couch, coffee table, etc). Large over-sized windows on the Eastern exposure flood the hardwood floors with natural light and provide fascinating views of the Brooklyn Bridge, South St Seaport, and surrounding waterscape. Settled on Water St, you are surrounded by an endless amount of options in terms of fine dining, shopping, culture and activities. South St Seaport is across the street from your doorsteps. 55 Fulton Market is conveniently located one block to the North. Titanic Memorial Park is across the street. NYC's historic Financial District and all its attractions are a five minute walk to the South-West. Transit options surround the area, with the primary hub being the newly renovated Fulton Street Station (operational 24 hours a day) where you can catch eight different train lines (including 2, 3, 4, 5, A, C, J, Z).
